notes: Maps source-dataset class labels to our frozen style_ids. Many-to-one is allowed (several source classes can collapse to one style_id). Source classes not listed here are dropped and their images go to rejects.csv. Spot-check N=20 per mapped class before accepting — noisy sources (Hairstyle30k was scraped from search engines) will have wrong labels that only human review catches.

hairstyle30k._notes: Hairstyle30k has 64 classes (2017 paper). The full class list is not shipped in the paper; names below are inferred from common labelings and the simplified 10-class variant. Update when the raw label files are downloaded.

k_hairstyle._notes: K-Hairstyle has 31 classes with Korean names in the official release. Fill this in after downloading the class list from the request-form response. Leaving placeholders so the ingest script fails loudly if an unmapped class arrives.

hairstyle40._notes: FaceSketches-HairStyle40 (HuggingFace yikaiwang/FaceSketches-HairStyle40). 40 class folders under image/. Refined subset of Hairstyle30k. Unmapped classes fall through to rejects.

scraped._notes: Web-scraped editorial portraits. Folder name IS the style_id (dataset_ingest.py handles this directly — no mapping lookup).

celebamask_hq._notes: CelebAMask-HQ provides hair segmentation, not style labels. Not used for style classification; only used in preprocessing for the hair-region mask model. No mapping needed.

reject_reasons.ambiguous: Label could plausibly be multiple style_ids; needs human review.
reject_reasons.no_mapping: Source class does not correspond to any style_id in our taxonomy.
reject_reasons.low_quality: Image is blurry, occluded, or not a single-person portrait.
reject_reasons.duplicate: Appears to duplicate another image in the dataset.
